PS. Dear Dev, please give us more experience like this.  How about Godzilla or King kong next?

reviews.review_title: I cant even watch the expirence
reviews.review_description: I just got scammed but I first got this jurassic park thing because I really like jurassic world but instead this 1 hour long download thing.

This is so stupid 2 videos of a dino waking up eats a leaf and sleeps
And then 2 blue is next to you roars and you need to download each video waste of time

This is so stupid. Like I thought this was a really fun Jurassic world game but it was more like a “watch 2 boring videos in 360°. There’s 2 videos, the first one is just a dinosaur waking up, looking at us, eating a leaf and going back to sleep for freaking 3 minutes. And the second is better but you cnat do anything you just watch, no gameplay just 360° videos.
Waste of TIME!!!

Looking around was choppy. Rather than a smooth change of perspective it was a jump each time you pivoted so you had no way of focusing on the area you wanted right in front of you. I hoped to watch an adventure with a storyline but 3 and 7 minutes with long credits included was dull. The background areas are a bit blurry. The only part I liked was having the dinosaurs faces come close to my face. I hope people did not have to pay money to watch these 2 clips.

For the more stupid users among us, I repeat that THIS IS NOT A GAME, but a 360° VR video experience! In any case, you have to DOWNLOAD both videos instead of STREAMING (the option is available after launching the application), because the streaming option will be very choppy and the result will be blurry. I'm already looking forward to showing it to my relatives and friends, because I automatically have the need to flinch in front of those big saurians :oD
This is not a game, just a movie.

I thought this would be a walk around where I would see various dinosaurs.. in fact it was a film showing a couple of dinosaurs… very disappointed with it. Would love to find an app where I can walk around during the Jurassic period and see dinosaurs up close..

I was kind of annoyed because I was under the assumption that it was a game or some kind of tour through Jurassic Park but it's just two videos that you just look around 360° and it's dumb and I'm mad I wasted my money on it
Very stupid
Not what I was thinking.

reviews.review_title: fuzzy low resolution what a shame,
reviews.review_description: Very fuzzy low resolution to the point you can’t make out much detail on anything,  leaves on trees are just a fuzzy mess and it affects the whole experience. It is a crying shame because this could be amazing. I would happily pay a few quid for this in a remastered version at  higher resolution and give it five stars. As it stands, I can’t give it more than one star. Completely ruined.

reviews.review_title: I Love Anything by Felix & Paul Studios
reviews.review_description: Felix & Paul Studios are amazing at their craft. I look forward to seeing and experiencing everything they create. I enjoyed the Jurassic World, Cirque du Soleil and President Barack Obama & family tour and history of the White House (a place/state I’ve never been), immensely! I thank them for their hard work and dedication perfecting the experience. Very much appreciated!

reviews.review_title: If you love Dinos and suspense.
reviews.review_description:
Jurassic World Aftermath doesn’t just deliver on suspense—it does it with a unique, roguelike-inspired art style that totally works. The cel-shaded graphics give it a bold, clean look that sets it apart from other VR titles. And honestly? It makes the game even scarier. The sharp visuals make every raptor movement pop, and the lighting creates this eerie vibe that keeps you constantly on edge.

It’s like Alien: Isolation got reimagined in this slick, stylized world, but the fear and tension are cranked all the way up. The design doesn’t distract—it pulls you in. It’s proof that hyper-realism isn’t the only way to make VR immersive. This game knows its strengths and absolutely leans into them.

If you’re into roguelike graphics paired with survival horror gameplay, Jurassic World Aftermath hits the sweet spot. It’s stylish, tense, and absolutely unforgettable.

How often do you get to sit and watch dinosaurs in their natural habitat for free? This is a great EXPERIENCE, not a game! My boy loves this and always asks to have a go again. If you have kids show them this, the first video not scary, the second could be.

Love the Jurassic Park/World franchise. When I saw the app, I immediately got it. Downloaded one of the films and hit play, but all I got was a red screen and the audio. I exited the app and went back in thinking it was a simple glitch, but the same thing happened. Don’t know if it’s just me or not.
Even after downloading the videos I still didn't have the resolution my headset offers. And for some odd reason this app wont allow me to watch from my phone when I let people play this game

The immersion is interesting, but the low resolution ruins the experience.

I recommend this video only for those who want to experiment -3 diopters of myopia.

I love dinosaurs but I really enjoy the Jurassic Park/World movies I even watched the shows Camp Cretaceous and Chaos Theory and played Jurassic World Evolution and Jurassic World Aftermath. I also I went to the Jurassic World Live Tour show by Field Entertainment and I collect the toys made by Mattel. But anyway let’s talk about the experience Jurassic World 2024. I really like the one with Blue the velociraptor I think it takes place a little while before the events of the movie Jurassic World Fallen Kingdom because of the volcano and the helicopters arriving. By the way Jurassic World Fallen Kingdom is my favorite Jurassic World movie. I kinda like the Apatosaurus one but it’s pretty boring.
Well I can say that Jurassic World 2024 is a good experience for Jurassic Park/World fans even if you’re not a fan of dinosaurs or Jurassic I bet you’re still going to enjoy it. One more thing I recommend the game Jurassic World Aftermath it is on Quest.
